Idiomatic Expressions

  • 巧令三则 - Title indicating three examples of clever actions or strategies.
  • 攫其居停误征已免钱粮印簿讹控者 - Complex phrase describing a situation where a guest in someone's house mistakenly claims exemption from taxes based on an incorrect document.
  • 状已上达，簿未呈堂 - Indicates that the complaint has been reported to a higher authority, but the document has not yet been presented in court.
  • 宪司咸以此人铁据在握，难以理断 - Suggests that the officials believe the complainant has solid evidence and that it is difficult to resolve the case.
  • 其能者不过私与往还，问其所欲，图赎其簿销之而已 - Describes how capable officials might privately negotiate with the complainant to resolve the matter.
  • 此人索数千金，不得丝毫缺 - Indicates that the complainant demands a large sum of money and will not accept any less.
  • 倘抱赃出首，中人亦连累无涯矣 - Suggests that if the complainant were to turn in the stolen goods, others involved would also be implicated.
  • 皆窘于计 - Means that everyone is at a loss for what to do.
  • 有巧令在侧微笑 - Describes a clever official who smiles when asked about the situation.
  • 若交职，三日办矣 - The official promises to resolve the issue within three days.
  • 不动声色 - Means to act without showing emotion or giving away one's intentions.
  • 限期已到，尚未佥票，得毋误乎 - Indicates that the deadline has passed, but no action has been taken, raising concerns about a mistake.
  • 余几忘之 - The official pretends to have forgotten about the matter.
  • 唤三班役来前曰 - The official calls three groups of officials to the front.
  • 有善斗殴者否 - Asks if there is anyone skilled in fighting.
  • 强项者出曰 - A bold individual volunteers to take on the task.
  • 汝能，必知我意 - The official confirms that the volunteer understands his intentions.
  • 今某处有幕客某寓，汝往生事激之斗 - Instructs the volunteer to go to a specific place and provoke a fight.
  • 但许自伤，勿许伤人 - Orders the volunteer to ensure that the fight is self-inflicted and not harmful to others.
  • 受伤即来鸣冤 - Instructs the volunteer to report the injury to the authorities.
  • 役曰唯 - The volunteer agrees to the instructions.
  • 即赴客寓，向其人大呼曰 - The volunteer goes to the guest's residence and loudly accuses him of owing money for transportation.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 幕客 - Refers to a guest or visitor staying in someone's house.
  • 居停 - Refers to the host or landlord.
  • 钱粮印簿 - Refers to official documents related to taxes and finances.
  • 宪司 - Refers to officials in charge of law and order.
  • 三班役 - Refers to groups of officials or servants.
  • 强项者 - Refers to a bold or stubborn individual.
  • 生事激之斗 - Refers to provoking a fight or conflict.
  • 自伤 - Refers to self-inflicted injuries.
  • 鸣冤 - Refers to reporting an injury or grievance to the authorities.

  • 巧令 - The name of the clever official.
  • 幕客 - Refers to the guest or visitor.
  • 居停 - Refers to the host or landlord.
  • 钱粮印簿 - Refers to official documents related to taxes and finances.
  • 宪司 - Refers to officials in charge of law and order.
  • 三班役 - Refers to groups of officials or servants.
  • 强项者 - Refers to the bold or stubborn individual.
  • 生事激之斗 - Refers to provoking a fight or conflict.
  • 自伤 - Refers to self-inflicted injuries.
  • 鸣冤 - Refers to reporting an injury or grievance to the authorities.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 客大怒曰 - Standard formula for introducing a character’s speech.
  • 何来恶棍，敢肆讹诈 - Strong accusation using classical rhetoric.
  • 役詈不已 - The official kept cursing or abusing. *詈* is a strong term for verbal abuse.
  • 推之出 - Pushed him out – a physical act of expulsion.
  • 自伤其首 - Injured his own head – a dramatic gesture to show self-harm or provocation.
  • 县令未退堂，即呼冤入跪 - The county magistrate had not yet left the court, but immediately called the accused to kneel and plead guilty. *未退堂* indicates the magistrate was still in session.
  • 具禀 - Submitted a formal report or made a detailed report. *禀* is a classical term for reporting to a superior.
  • 与所书单无异 - Was exactly the same as the list he had written. *无异* means 'no difference.'
  • 行箧中 - In the traveler's suitcase or baggage. *行箧* is a compound word for luggage.
  • 印簿 - Imperial register or official document. Likely refers to an official record or document with an official seal.
  • 何得私自携取 - How could you have taken it privately? *携取* means to take or carry away.
  • 姑全颜面 - I will spare your face or dignity. A euphemistic way of saying leniency.
  • 命取火焚之 - Ordered fire to be brought to burn it. *命取火* is a command to get fire, and *焚之* is to burn it.
  • 案已上控 - The case has already been reported upward or filed with higher authorities. *上控* is a legal term for reporting a case to higher authorities.
  • 手快 - Quick hands – a colloquial term for someone who acts quickly, often in a sneaky or greedy way.
  • 天道好还 - The way of heaven favors return or the moral order of heaven brings about retribution. A classical moral saying.
  • 第我治下不容奸险之徒 - But in my jurisdiction, I will not tolerate deceitful and dangerous people. *第* here is used as a conjunction meaning 'but' or 'however.'
  • 即备文递回原籍可也 - I will prepare a document and send him back to his native place. *备文* is to prepare a written document.
  • 隐忍吞声而去 - Left in silence, swallowing his anger. *隐忍* means to endure in silence, *吞声* means to suppress one's voice or complaints.
  • 缴卷销案 - Submitted the case file and closed the case. *缴卷* is to submit a case file, *销案* is to close the case.
  • 宪司优奖之 - The provincial governor gave him commendation and reward. *宪司* refers to the provincial administration or governor.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 县令 - Refers to the county magistrate, a local official in the imperial Chinese bureaucracy.
  • 役 - Refers to a county official or clerk, often of lower rank, sometimes with a derogatory connotation in classical texts.
  • 印簿 - Likely refers to an official register or document with an official seal, possibly a tax or legal record.
  • 上控 - A formal term used in classical Chinese legal terminology to report a case to higher authorities for review.
  • 宪司 - Refers to the provincial administrative office or the governor, who had higher authority than the county magistrate.
  • 隐忍吞声 - A classical expression describing the act of enduring injustice or insult in silence, often used in moral or historical narratives.
  • 天道好还 - A classical moral saying, implying that justice will be served in due time, often used in stories of retribution.
  • 奸险之徒 - Refers to deceitful or dangerous individuals, a term used to describe morally corrupt or untrustworthy people.

Proper Nouns

  • 客 - The guest – a generic term, not a proper name.
  • 役 - The official – a generic term, not a proper name.
  • 县令 - The county magistrate – a title, not a name.
  • 宪司 - The provincial administration – a title, not a name.
  • 印簿 - Imperial register – a term referring to an official document, not a proper name.

  • 图赖 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'to blame' or 'to accuse falsely,' suggesting that if the person sees Liu, they might try to shift the blame onto him.

  • 感激涕零而去 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'deeply grateful and teary-eyed, and left,' describing Liu's emotional reaction and departure.
  • 李亦从此悔过矣 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'Li has repented and changed his ways from this point onward,' indicating that Li has repented.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 此以人就联也 - Phrase suggesting 'bringing people together' or 'aligning people with a group'; verb 就 means 'to approach, align with, or join,' and 联 refers to a collective or group.
  • 胖大和尚可证 - Metaphorical or illustrative statement using a specific character (a fat monk) to exemplify or prove a broader point.

  • 引道生魂 - Idiomatic and metaphorical phrase; 引道 could mean 'to lead toward the path,' and 生魂 may mean 'to give life to the soul' or 'to revive the spirit.'

  • 弥勒种因 - Refers to 'the cause of Maitreya's cultivation'; 弥勒 is Maitreya, the future Buddha in Mahayana Buddhism, and 种因 means 'to plant the seed of cause' or 'to cultivate the cause.'
  • 王君结果也 - Phrase linking a previous cause (Maitreya's cultivation) to a specific outcome involving Lord Wang; 结果 means 'to bear fruit' or 'to achieve a result.'
  • 其然乎？ - Rhetorical question meaning 'Is that the case?' or 'Could it be so?'
  • 圆谎先生 - Humorous or satirical title suggesting a person who is good at smoothing over or justifying lies.

  • 打诳语 - Colloquial expression for someone who habitually lies or tells untruths.
  • 率荒诞不经 - Means 'generally absurd and without foundation'; 率 means 'generally,' 荒诞 means 'absurd' or 'nonsensical,' and 不经 means 'unfounded' or 'not grounded in reality.'
  • 几谏不从 - Classical expression meaning 'repeatedly advised but not heeded'; 几谏 means 'to advise repeatedly,' and 不从 means 'not to follow' or 'not to listen.'
  • 岁修数百金 - Refers to 'an annual stipend of several hundred gold pieces'; 岁修 refers to an annual salary or stipend, and 金 is a unit of currency, typically gold.
  • 淳于之流 - Refers to someone in the style or tradition of 淳于, likely a reference to 淳于髡 (Chunyu Kun), a famous figure from the Warring States period known for his wit and eloquence.
  • 朝岁勿离左右 - Formal expression indicating that the person is to be constantly at the side of the old man; 朝岁 means 'day or night,' and 勿离左右 means 'not to be separated from his side.'

  • 无柄巨斧，奚能浮江 - Rhetorical question questioning how a large axe without a handle could float on the river. '奚能' means 'how could.'
  • 戏具中木斧耳 - Suggests that the axe might be a toy made of wood. '戏具' means 'toy,' and '木斧' means 'wooden axe.'
  • 老夫亦诧，辨之实镔铁所为者 - The old man expresses his surprise and insists that the axe was indeed made of steel. '老夫' is a respectful term for an old man, '诧' means 'surprised,' and '镔铁' means 'steel.'
  • 群笑其谬 - Indicates that the group laughed at the old man's mistake. '群' means 'the crowd,' and '谬' means 'mistake' or 'error.'
  • 毋哗 - Command to stop making noise. '毋' is a negative imperative, and '哗' means 'noise' or 'commotion.'
  • 总而言之曰墙 - Summary statement indicating that regardless of the material, it is still called a wall. '总而言之' means 'in summary,' and '曰' means 'is called.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 翁 - Refers to an elderly man, often used to denote respect.
  • 先生 - A respectful term for a scholar or teacher.
  • 邻翁 - Refers to a neighbor who is also an elderly man.
  • 江畔 - Refers to the riverside, a common setting in classical Chinese literature.
  • 镔铁 - A type of steel, often used in ancient Chinese metallurgy.
  • 墙 - Refers to walls, which can be made of various materials but are generally called 'walls.'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '翁偕先生徘徊江畔，邻翁来接谈笑，共问翁曰：“近日有新闻否？”' describes the scene where the old man and the scholar are walking along the riverside, and a neighbor joins them in conversation. The dialogue is embedded within the narrative.
  • The sentence '翁曰：“有之，昨与先生游此，对江有人持数十觔铁斧斫树，脱落江中，浮而过，视之无柄。非异事乎？”' describes a recent event involving a large axe that fell into the river. The sentence structure is complex, with multiple clauses describing the event.
  • The sentence '邻翁曰：“无柄巨斧，奚能浮江？或戏具中木斧耳。”' questions the old man's account and suggests that the axe might be a toy.
  • The sentence '翁曰：“老夫亦诧，辨之实镔铁所为者。”' insists that the axe was made of steel.
  • The sentence '群笑其谬' indicates that the group laughed at the old man's mistake.
  • The sentence '先生曰：“非谬也，我昨同观，缘斧头吃入树枝，树本折，连枝叶浮而过此。”' clarifies the situation, explaining that the axe head got stuck in a tree branch, causing the tree to fall and float downstream.
  • The sentence '邻翁无词以诘' indicates that the neighbor has no further argument.
  • The sentence '或曰：“昨夜风甚大，真有偃禾拔木之势。”' mentions that the previous night had a strong wind that could uproot trees.
  • The sentence '翁曰：“不仅此也，敝园有井，竟被风吹出墙外。”' adds that his garden well was blown out of the wall by the wind.
  • The sentence '众皆大哗' indicates that everyone is surprised and makes a commotion.
  • The sentence '先生曰：“毋哗，主人园中，墙不一类，有砖石筑者，有紫竹结者，有荆条编者，总而言之曰墙。”' calms everyone down and explains that the garden walls are made of different materials but are still called walls.

  • 有友入其斋，先生不在 - Narrative about a friend entering the scholar's study while he was absent; '有友入其斋' (yǒu yǒu rù qí zhāi) means 'a friend entered his study,' and '先生不在' (xiān shēng bù zài) means 'the master was not there.'

  • 先生归，查籍记事，见敦伦处俱加一点 - Description of the scholar's reaction upon returning; '先生归' (xiān shēng guī) means 'the master returned,' '查籍记事' (chá jí jì shì) means 'checked the records,' and '见敦伦处俱加一点' (jiàn dūn lún chù jù jiā yī diǎn) means 'saw that all the places mentioning marital relations were marked with a dot.'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The phrase '十余男妇以斗米眉豚，叩门而拜' describes a group of people bringing a modest offering and bowing, but the exact nature of the offering and the act of bowing can be ambiguous.
  • The sentence '母惶恐不自安，曰：“何为乎？”' introduces the mother's reaction and her question, but the context of her anxiety and the specific question can be unclear.
  • The phrase '来者众皆稽首而告曰' describes the visitors bowing and speaking, but the exact content of their message can be ambiguous.
  • The clause '汝等既知感戴，此后惟吾言是听，当福汝' presents a conditional statement, but the exact relationship between gratitude and obedience can be unclear.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '是日，宦家有召巫者，即不信神言之某某，正表扬主家隐事' is elliptical and grammatically compact. It can be read as 'On that day, a court official summoned a witch, who was a person who did not believe in divine words, and was praising the host family's hidden matters.'
  • The sequence of actions in '众方环观耸听，忽自掴其口，伏地诉曰：“神责我不应贿通某媪，生事诳言。着自击面一百。”' must be read as: the crowd was watching and listening intently, when the witch suddenly slapped her own mouth, fell to the ground, and confessed, 'The gods are blaming me for bribing a certain old woman, causing trouble and lying. I should strike my face a hundred times.'
  • The phrase '两手自责，口告饶命' describes the witch's actions: she struck herself with both hands and pleaded for mercy.
  • The phrase '又曰：“小人就至神巫家请罪。”狂奔而去' means the witch then said, 'I, a lowly person, will go to the witch's house to confess my sins,' and ran away wildly.
  • The phrase '主家大笑，诘媪，不敢隐，亦自服其辜' means the host family laughed loudly, questioned the old woman, who dared not hide anything, and she also admitted her guilt.
  • The phrase '遂撤其供献，及应给之财帛，亦至母家投纳' means as a result, the offerings were removed, and the appropriate financial and material gifts were also submitted to the mother's family.
  • The phrase '母正默坐时，忽见狰狞鬼卒押一人，头脸红肿，至母前跪拜请罪' means the mother was sitting in silence when she suddenly saw fierce ghost soldiers escorting one person with a swollen face and head, who knelt before her and begged for forgiveness.
  • The phrase '正皇惑间，又见某绅率家人妇女盛送礼物，且为某巫跪求忏悔' means while in a state of confusion, she also saw a certain gentleman leading his family and women to bring generous gifts, and he knelt to seek repentance for a certain witch.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 孝子家本盛族 - Formal statement indicating that the filial son comes from a once-prosperous family.
  • 以读书出仕者不乏人 - Means 'there are many who have risen to officialdom through studying,' referring to the family's historical prominence.
  • 因其子母业鄙，宗人不甚往来 - Concessive clause explaining that the family's fortunes have declined, leading to estrangement from relatives.
  • 今母如是之盛，且身分自居 - Means 'now that the mother has regained such prosperity, and she has elevated herself,' indicating a reversal of fortune.
  • 而入争奉之 - Means 'people now compete to serve her,' highlighting the sudden change in the mother's status.
  • 故其等夷及卑幼辈皆来趋奉 - Means 'so those of her own rank and the younger generation all come to serve,' emphasizing the social dynamics.
  • 亦有所觊觎耳 - Means 'they also have some ulterior motives,' suggesting that the serving is not entirely sincere.
  • 睹其景象光昌 - Means 'seeing her prosperous scene,' describing the mother's current status.
  • 竟分日问安侍膳 - Means 'they even take turns to inquire about her well-being and serve her meals,' indicating the extent of the service.
  • 母于富贵者礼之，贫贱者济之 - Means 'the mother treats the wealthy with courtesy and helps the poor,' showing her benevolence.
  • 举族悦服 - Means 'the whole family is pleased and submissive,' indicating the family's acceptance of the mother's authority.
  • 族长欲为择继 - Means 'the clan leader wants to choose a successor,' referring to the mother's potential remarriage.
  • 因立贤立爱，争执未定 - Means 'because of the conflict between choosing the virtuous or the beloved, the decision is still undecided,' highlighting the dilemma.
  • 儿奉母又十余年矣 - Means 'I have served you for more than ten years,' indicating the duration of the filial son's care.
  • 天下无不散之筵席 - Metaphor meaning 'all good things must come to an end,' referring to the inevitability of separation.
  • 今母年八旬，既寿且康 - Means 'now that you are eighty years old, you are both long-lived and healthy,' describing the mother's condition.
  • 奈儿假满不得再留人间 - Means 'but I am about to complete my term and cannot stay in the human world any longer,' indicating the filial son's impending departure.
  • 盍先为母刨基业于地下 - Means 'why not first establish a foundation for you in the underworld,' suggesting a plan for the mother's future.
  • 然后奉母西归，不亦乐乎 - Means 'and then take you to the West, wouldn't that be delightful?' proposing a solution.
  • 但冥间重者诰命 - Means 'but in the afterlife, the most important thing is the imperial decree,' emphasizing the need for official recognition.
  • 而先人亦无承祀 - Means 'and the ancestors have no one to continue the ancestral rites,' highlighting the need for a successor.
  • 儿阴择得族弟诸生某，长厚而有福泽，可为父后 - Means 'I have secretly chosen a cousin who is a scholar, mature and kind, with blessings, and can be your successor,' introducing the chosen successor.
  • 已密遣赴都谋功名，不日事备 - Means 'I have secretly sent him to the capital to seek fame and glory, and soon everything will be ready,' indicating the plan's progress.
  • 请母于热闹场中归去，受冥途清福何如 - Means 'please allow me to take you to the bustling scene and return to the afterlife, to enjoy the clear blessings of the underworld,' proposing the final step.

  • 与其郁郁生，何如堂堂死耶 - A rhetorical question expressing that it is better to die with dignity than to live in misery.

  • 凡二三日内雨至，先必落水 - Description of a pattern; '凡二三日内雨至' means 'within two or three days before the rain comes'; '先必落水' means 'the fish will first fall into the water.'
  • 子述告示之日，适咸鱼落水之时，故信之确 - Statement of certainty; '子述告示之日' means 'when you reported the edict'; '适咸鱼落水之时' means 'just when the fish fell into the water'; '故信之确' means 'so I believed it was true.'
  • 今亦不难，子持此鱼至省，悬于卧内 - Plan of action; '今亦不难' means 'it is not difficult now'; '子持此鱼至省' means 'you bring this fish to the province'; '悬于卧内' means 'hang it in your room.'
  • 以先贤董仲舒五龙祈雨之法，铺张陈设 - Reference to historical method; '先贤董仲舒' means 'the ancient scholar Dong Zhongshu'; '五龙祈雨之法' means 'the method of five dragons for praying for rain'; '铺张陈设' means 'to set up elaborately.'
  • 若鱼干无水，总以坛不如式，器用不全，频使改作，以延时日 - Strategy to delay; '若鱼干无水' means 'if the fish is dry and no water'; '总以坛不如式' means 'always claim the altar is not properly set up'; '器用不全' means 'instruments are incomplete'; '频使改作' means 'frequently require changes'; '以延时日' means 'to delay time.'
  • 若一得鱼水，即登坛诵经，未有不获者 - Plan of action; '若一得鱼水' means 'if the fish is in water'; '即登坛诵经' means 'immediately ascend the altar and recite scriptures'; '未有不获者' means 'no one fails to get what they want.'
  • 何怨之有 - Rhetorical question meaning 'what reason to resent?'

  • 及赴省会，则鱼已汗淋 - Description of the situation; '及赴省会' means 'when he went to the provincial capital'; '则鱼已汗淋' means 'the fish was already sweating.'
  • 急谒大府，朝登坛而夕如注矣 - Description of the action; '急谒大府' means 'hurriedly went to see the high official'; '朝登坛而夕如注矣' means 'ascended the altar in the morning and it rained heavily by evening.'

Proper Nouns

  • 翁 - The old man.
  • 其妻女 - His wife and daughters.
  • 猫 - The cat.
  • 井上之李 - The plums on the well.
  • 廉士 - A righteous person.
  • 殉葬物 - Funerary objects.
  • 复壁承尘 - Hidden compartments in walls and ceilings.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 財可忽乎哉 - An emphatic rhetorical question meaning 'Can wealth be neglected?' It emphasizes the importance of wealth.
  • 庭帏行孝 - Refers to performing filial piety within the family, specifically in the context of the home (庭) and the inner chambers (帏).
  • 棣萼联情 - Uses the metaphor of branches of a tree (棣萼) to describe close familial ties or sibling relationships.
  • 爵可得而鬻焉，刑可得而赎焉 - These phrases use the verb 鬻 (sell) and 赎 (redeem) to indicate that positions of power (爵) can be bought and punishments (刑) can be paid off.
  • 以之救济，仁名顿起；以之施与，传为美谈 - These phrases highlight the moral and social benefits of using wealth for charity and generosity.
  • 彼猫乌知之，亦以此取义，且永锡尔类，岂不异哉 - A metaphorical statement comparing the understanding of wealth and its use to a cat, implying that even animals can understand the value of wealth and its ethical implications.
  • 恒山李老，农家者流 - Describes Li Lao as a farmer from the Hengshan region.
  • 有地数顷，称小康 - Indicates that Li Lao has several acres of land and is considered moderately well-off.
  • 稍长附学读书，督课极严 - Describes how Li Lao's son, Yi, was educated with strict supervision.
  • 窃资逃去 - Means 'stole money and fled,' indicating that Yi ran away after stealing money.
  • 悬金以购，搜索无所不至，迄无影响 - Describes the extensive search for Yi, with no success.
  • 年齿正强，可望生育慰之 - Suggests that Li Lao hopes that his wife will have more children to comfort him.
  • 妇已思子成疾，屡劝置妾延嗣，李老不忍 - Describes how Li Lao's wife fell ill due to missing her son and repeatedly suggested taking a concubine to have more children, but Li Lao refused.
  • 光阴迅速，瞬逾十年 - Indicates that time passed quickly, and ten years went by.
  • 年将古稀，仍无所出 - States that Li Lao is approaching seventy years old and still has no children.
  • 宗族之贫者咸思争继，哓哓不休，益厌苦之 - Describes how poor relatives of Li Lao are vying for inheritance, causing distress.
  • 自度精力尚强，且值旱涝不匀之岁，闻韩魏间售子女者，直甚廉 - Indicates that Li Lao, feeling his energy is still strong, decides to buy a daughter during a year of uneven drought and floods when prices are low.
  • 携百金往投人牙，以清钱五十贯择得端庄少女，大称心怀 - Describes how Li Lao took a hundred gold pieces to buy a daughter, selecting a modest and well-behaved girl for fifty strings of cash, which greatly pleased him.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 恒山 - Refers to Hengshan, a mountain range in China.
  • 农家者流 - Refers to a farmer or someone from a farming background.
  • 庭帏 - Refers to the inner chambers of a house, often associated with family life and domestic affairs.
  • 棣萼 - A metaphor for close familial ties, derived from the branches of a tree.
  • 韩魏间 - Refers to the regions of Han and Wei, which were historical areas in China.
  • 人牙 - Refers to a matchmaker or broker who arranges the sale of people, often used in the context of buying and selling slaves or servants.
  • 清钱五十贯 - Refers to a specific amount of money, where '贯' (guan) is a unit of currency.
  • 端庄少女 - Describes a modest and well-behaved young woman.

Proper Nouns

  • 恒山 - The name of a mountain range in China.
  • 李老 - The name of the farmer.
  • 壹 - The name of Li Lao's son.
  • 韩魏 - Historical regions in China.
  • 清钱 - A unit of currency.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 叩翁姓名籍贯 - Respectfully inquiring about the old man's name and hometown.
  • 实告之 - Telling her the truth.
  • 异哉 - Exclamation meaning 'strange' or 'unusual.'
  • 路隔五百里 - Separated by five hundred miles.
  • 云系李姓名壹 - Said to be named Li Yi.
  • 恒山人 - A person from Hengshan.
  • 因逃学出 - Because he ran away from school.
  • 为人义子 - To be an adopted son.
  • 亲父母在乎否乎 - Are your biological parents still alive?
  • 念诵涕泣 - To recite and cry.
  • 与母时慰劝之 - Sometimes comfort and advise her.
  • 据汝言，确是吾子矣 - According to what you say, it is certainly my son.
  • 当为吾孙女 - Should be my granddaughter.
  • 幸言之早 - Fortunately you mentioned it early.
  • 速赴尔家验之 - Quickly go to your home to verify it.
  • 虽相隔十余年 - Even though separated for more than ten years.
  • 声音笑貌应不改也 - The voice and appearance should not have changed.
  • 孤苦伶仃抱病 - Suffering from illness and being lonely and destitute.
  • 不觉跃然而起 - Unconsciously jump up and stand up.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 恒山 - A famous mountain in China, one of the Five Great Mountains.
  • 逃学 - Running away from school, a common issue in traditional Chinese society.

Proper Nouns

  • 李老 - The name of the elderly man.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 以词林壮年告休 - Classical phrase meaning 'to retire from a distinguished position in one's prime.'
  • 手不释卷 - Fixed expression meaning 'to never put down a book,' indicating constant study.
  • 无著作 - Literally means 'no writings,' but in this context, it emphasizes that Tchen Bo did not produce any written works.
  • 终身读书而已 - Means 'to read books all one's life,' emphasizing the simplicity and dedication of his lifestyle.
  • 司铎府学 - Refers to serving as a teacher in the official school of the government.
  • 髦而致仕归 - Means 'to retire at an advanced age and return home.'
  • 孜孜 - Means 'diligently' or 'perseveringly.'
  • 进而规之曰 - Means 'to advise further, saying,' indicating a more serious or formal tone in the advice given.
  • 予非博髦而好学之名 - Means 'I do not seek the reputation of being an elderly scholar who loves learning,' expressing humility and a lack of interest in fame.
  • 予耻不识字也 - Means 'I am ashamed not to know characters,' indicating a deep sense of shame or guilt about not being literate.
  • 何至于是 - Means 'Why should it come to this?' expressing surprise or disbelief.
  • 多或不能，数千字可识 - Means 'I might not know many, but I can recognize several thousand characters,' indicating a limited but significant level of literacy.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 陈氏，海昌世家 - Refers to the Chen family, a prominent family from Hai Chang, a region known for its scholarly tradition.
  • 词林 - Refers to the literary world or the place where scholars gather, often used to denote a prestigious position in the imperial examination system.
  • 壮年告休 - Indicates a nobleman or official retiring from a high position during his prime years, a common practice in ancient China.
  • 进士 - Refers to a scholar who has passed the highest level of the imperial examinations, eligible for high-ranking government positions.
  • 司铎府学 - Refers to serving as a teacher in the official school of the government, a respected role in society.
  • 髦而致仕归 - Indicates retirement at an advanced age, a common practice among scholars and officials in ancient China.
  • 进而规之曰 - Suggests a more serious or formal tone in the advice given, indicating a deeper level of concern or guidance.

Proper Nouns

  • 陈伯 - The name of the elder brother.
  • 陈氏 - The surname of the family.
  • 海昌 - A region known for its scholarly tradition.
  • 词林 - A term referring to the literary world or a prestigious position in the imperial examination system.
  • 壮年 - Refers to the prime of one's life.
  • 告休 - A term for retiring from a position.
  • 进士 - A scholar who has passed the highest level of the imperial examinations.
  • 司铎府学 - A term for serving as a teacher in the official school of the government.
  • 髦而致仕归 - A term for retiring at an advanced age and returning home.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 伯曰 - Means 'the elder brother said,' a common way to introduce a speaker in classical Chinese.
  • 仲曰 - Means 'the middle brother said,' another way to introduce a speaker.
  • 三岁幼子尚识之 - Literally 'a three-year-old child still knows it,' used to emphasize that the knowledge is basic and fundamental.
  • 非敢轻汝 - Means 'it is not that I am belittling you,' a polite way to express that the speaker is not insulting the listener.
  • 字实难识 - Means 'the character is truly difficult to recognize,' emphasizing the complexity of the character.
  • 凡字有五声，有八法，有实读，有虚读 - Means 'all characters have five tones, eight strokes, actual readings, and virtual readings,' a comprehensive statement about the complexity of Chinese characters.
  • 实读始于何书 - Means 'actual reading begins in which book,' asking about the origin of the actual reading of a character.
  • 虚读亦然 - Means 'virtual reading is also the same,' indicating that virtual reading follows a similar pattern to actual reading.
  • 五声辩之 - Means 'distinguish by five tones,' referring to the five tones in Chinese phonology.
  • 八法书之 - Means 'write by eight strokes,' referring to the eight basic strokes in Chinese calligraphy.
  • 真草隶篆 - Refers to the four main styles of Chinese calligraphy: regular script, cursive script, clerical script, and seal script.
  • 变体亦无所不知 - Means 'even variant forms are known without exception,' emphasizing comprehensive knowledge of all forms of a character.
  • 方可曰我识此字 - Means 'only then can one say that they know this character,' indicating that true knowledge of a character requires comprehensive understanding.
  • 见于经史子集者共有若干 - Means 'how many are found in the classics, histories, philosophical works, and literary collections,' asking about the frequency of a character's appearance in various texts.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 十三经 - Refers to the Thirteen Classics, a collection of Confucian texts.
  • 十七史 - Refers to the Seventeen Histories, a collection of historical texts.
  • 说部子集 - Refers to the four categories of Chinese literature: novels, philosophical works, historical works, and literary collections.
  • 葛天、刑天 - Refers to historical state names, indicating cultural and historical knowledge.
  • 汉令天高 - Refers to historical family names, indicating cultural and historical knowledge.

Proper Nouns

  • 伯 - The elder brother.
  • 仲 - The middle brother.
  • 天 - The character being discussed.
  • 十三经 - The Thirteen Classics.

  • 说部子集 - The four categories of Chinese literature.
  • 葛天 - A historical state name.
  • 刑天 - A historical state name.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 皆当实读 - Should be read literally or taken at face value.
  • 此不过言其大略 - This is just a general overview.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 天根、天枢、天棓 - Specific star names in Chinese astronomy.
  • 天台、天方、天山 - Specific geographical locations in China.
  • 景天、烛天、天门冬 - Specific plant names in Chinese botany.
  • 漏天、木天、谈天 - Names that are read figuratively rather than literally.
  • 五方 - Refers to the five cardinal directions (north, south, east, west, and center).

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 若数典而备陈之 - Classical phrase meaning 'if one were to enumerate and fully detail everything.'
  • 终日不能毕一字 - Means 'one could not complete even one character in a whole day.'
  • 如此方为识字 - Means 'only in this way can one truly be said to know how to read.'
  • 人寿几何 - Phrase meaning 'how long is a person's life?'
  • 即逾百龄亦不及也 - Means 'even if one lived beyond a hundred years, it would still not be enough.'
  • 不如是，冒称识字是无耻矣 - Means 'if one does not do this, claiming to know how to read is a disgrace.'
  • 知识字之义乎 - Means 'do you understand the meaning of knowing how to read?'
  • 相识者 - Refers to someone who is known or acquainted with.
  • 必知其父祖是何名号 - Means 'one must know the names and titles of their ancestors.'
  • 母妻何氏 - Means 'the surname of their mother and wife.'
  • 兄弟几人 - Means 'how many brothers they have.'
  • 子孙何名何字 - Means 'the names and characters of their descendants.'
  • 作何事业 - Means 'what kind of career or achievements they have.'
  • 一一能详述之 - Means 'be able to detail each one.'
  • 方不愧为相识 - Means 'only then can one truly be called acquainted.'
  • 仅能辨其本身姓名声容笑貌 - Means 'if one can only recognize their own name, appearance, and mannerisms.'
  • 不过泛交耳 - Means 'it is merely a superficial acquaintance.'
  • 识云乎哉 - Means 'how can it be called knowing?'
  • 识字不外此理也 - Means 'knowing how to read is no different from this principle.'
  • 弟实不能，兄好为之 - Means 'I truly cannot, please do it for me, brother.'
  • 顿首服膺而退 - Means 'he bowed respectfully and left, having fully accepted the advice.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 识字 - Refers to the ability to read and write, a fundamental skill in Chinese culture.
  • 母妻何氏 - Refers to the surnames of the mother and wife, highlighting the importance of family connections.
  • 兄弟几人 - Refers to the number of siblings, indicating the family structure.
  • 子孙何名何字 - Refers to the names and characters of descendants, showing the continuity of family.
  • 作何事业 - Refers to career and achievements, reflecting the values of productivity and contribution.
  • 泛交 - Refers to a superficial or casual acquaintance, contrasting with a deep and meaningful relationship.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 若数典而备陈之，终日不能毕一字 - Hypothetical statement suggesting that even if one were to list everything, it would take an entire day to write just one character.
  • 如此方为识字？人寿几何，即逾百龄亦不及也 - Rhetorical question followed by a statement, emphasizing the vastness of knowledge and the limitations of human life.
  • 不如是，冒称识字是无耻矣 - Conditional statement suggesting that failing to meet the criteria for true literacy is shameful.
  • 知识字之义乎？ - Question asking about the deeper meaning of literacy.
  • 相识者，必知其父祖是何名号，母妻何氏，兄弟几人，子孙何名何字，作何事业，一一能详述之，方不愧为相识 - Detailed description of what it means to truly know someone, emphasizing the importance of knowing their family history and personal details.
  • 仅能辨其本身姓名声容笑貌，不过泛交耳，识云乎哉 - Contrast between superficial knowledge and true understanding.
  • 识字不外此理也 - Conclusion that the principle of true literacy applies to all areas of knowledge.
  • 弟实不能，兄好为之 - Request for help, indicating a lack of ability and a plea for assistance.
  • 顿首服膺而退 - Formal expression of respect and acceptance, indicating that the advice has been taken to heart.

[598]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 此考據家也 - Refers to scholars of textual research; introduces the topic without explicitly stating distinguishing features.
  • 以渊博为事 - Emphasizes the scholar's dedication to accumulating broad knowledge; somewhat abstract.
  • 有道学家，自太极两仪推而至周程张朱之理，无所不达 - Describes the development of Neo-Confucian thought from the *Yi Jing* to the teachings of Zhou, Cheng, Zhang, and Zhu; somewhat elliptical.
  • 有兵法家，韬略阴府，五花八门，攻守技艺，无不精练者 - Describes scholars of military strategy and their mastery of various techniques; somewhat elliptical.
  • 通才不一，惟圣人能兼之 - Suggests that true talent spans multiple disciplines; only sages can master all fields; somewhat elliptical.
  • 至于文章诗歌，润色太平，足以黼黻皇猷 - Introduces the topic of literature and poetry, emphasizing their role in enhancing society; somewhat elliptical.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 太极两仪 - Refers to the foundational concepts of the *Yi Jing*, describing the universe in terms of the Taiji and Yin-Yang.
  • 周程张朱 - Refers to prominent Neo-Confucian scholars: Zhou Dunyi, Cheng Hao, Cheng Yi, Zhang Zai, and Zhu Xi.
  • 黄老 - Refers to the Huang-Lao school, combining Daoism and Legalism, emphasizing the Yellow Emperor and Laozi.
  • 申韩萧曹 - Refers to key figures in the Qin and Han dynasties: Shen Buhai, Han Fei, Xiao He, and Cao Cao.
  • 河图洛书 - Ancient Chinese texts associated with the origins of the *Yi Jing* and divination, believed to have been revealed by the Yellow River and the Luo River.
  • 天文星度 - Refers to the study of astronomy and the movements of celestial bodies.
  • 奇门禽遁 - Likely refers to *Qi Men Dun Jia*, a form of Chinese divination.
  • 兵法家 - Refers to scholars of military strategy, such as Sun Tzu.
  • 韬略阴府 - Refers to the study of military strategy and covert operations.
  • 五花八门 - A metaphor for the diversity of military tactics.
  • 攻守技艺 - Refers to the skills involved in military combat, including both offensive and defensive techniques.
  • 鬼神莫测之机 - Suggests that these scholars have knowledge that is beyond ordinary understanding.
  • 为王者师 - Refers to the role of scholars in advising rulers.
  • 有益于天下后世者也 - Emphasizes the lasting impact of their work on society and future generations.
  • 通才不一 - Suggests that true talent spans multiple disciplines.
  • 惟圣人能兼之 - Implies that only the most exceptional individuals can master all fields of knowledge.
  • 文章诗歌 - Refers to literary and poetic achievement.
  • 润色太平 - Refers to the role of literature in enhancing the cultural and moral fabric of society.
  • 黼黻皇猷 - Refers to the adornment of imperial policies.
  • 固代有名家 - Acknowledges the historical presence of literary and artistic masters.
  • 医卜星相 - Refers to esoteric practices such as medicine, divination, astrology, and physiognomy.
  • 小道可观 - Suggests that while these fields are not as significant, they still have value.
  • 无大造于国家 - Emphasizes that these fields do not have the same impact on governance and society.
  • 卑卑不足法矣 - Suggests that these fields should not be taken as models for others to follow.

[599]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 壬戌之秋 - A classical dating system using the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches to denote the year and season.
  • 予應試武林 - "予" means "I," and "應試" means "to take an examination." "武林" is an ancient name for Hangzhou, a city in Zhejiang Province.
  • 寓室之前，隔河有染坊 - Describes a setting where the author's residence is across a river from a dyeing workshop.
  • 每晨數十人在河干练色 - "每晨" means "every morning," "數十人" means "dozens of people," "河干" means "by the riverbank," and "练色" means "dyeing silk colors."
  • 杵声与俚歌相答 - "杵声" refers to the sound of pounding, and "俚歌" refers to folk songs. "相答" means "echoing back and forth."
  • 讻讻惊睡 - "讻讻" means "noisy" or "chaotic," and "惊睡" means "disturbing sleep."
  • 作而询 - "作" means "to get up," and "询" means "to inquire."
  • 主人对曰 - "主人" means "the host" or "landlord," and "对曰" means "replied."
  • 是蒋氏，三十年前不过落寞小肆 - "是" means "this," "蒋氏" refers to the Jiang family, "三十年前" means "thirty years ago," "不过" means "was just," and "落寞小肆" means "a small and unremarkable shop."
  • 蒋翁人甚忠厚 - "蒋翁" means "Mr. Jiang," and "人甚忠厚" means "he was very honest and kind."
  • 生三子一女 - "生" means "had," "三子" means "three sons," and "一女" means "one daughter."
  • 女已嫁，长次子继父业 - "女已嫁" means "the daughter is married," "长次子" means "the eldest and second sons," and "继父业" means "carry on the father's business."
  • 三官尚幼，随父兄学习 - "三官" means "the third son," "尚幼" means "still young," and "随父兄学习" means "follow his father and elder brother in learning."
  • 未几，二子俱完姻 - "未几" means "not long after," "二子" means "the two sons," and "俱完姻" means "both got married."
  • 食指日繁 - "食指" means "number of dependents," and "日繁" means "increasing daily."
  • 本既不多，息亦无几 - "本" means "capital," "不多" means "not much," "息" means "interest," and "无几" means "very little."
  • 出入不敷，蒋翁以郁卒 - "出入不敷" means "income did not cover expenses," and "以郁卒" means "died of depression."
  • 三官无人管束，终日游荡 - "无人管束" means "no one to discipline," and "终日游荡" means "wandering all day."
  • 兄训之不悛，亦置度外矣 - "兄训之不悛" means "the elder brother scolded him but he did not reform," and "亦置度外矣" means "he was also ignored."
  • 姊怜之，其夫业贸丝 - "姊怜之" means "the elder sister pitied him," "其夫" means "her husband," and "业贸丝" means "engaged in silk trade."
  • 行中客俱嘉湖粤闽富商 - "行中客" means "travellers," "俱" means "all," "嘉湖粤闽" refers to Jiangxi, Huzhou, Guangdong, and Fujian provinces, and "富商" means "wealthy merchants."
  • 须人晋接，姊于是呼三官去日 - "须人晋接" means "needed someone to entertain guests," "姊于是呼三官去日" means "the sister then called the third son to go."
  • 汝游手好闲，何能自食 - "汝" means "you," "游手好闲" means "loafing around," and "何能自食" means "how can you support yourself."
  • 今我肆中客多，我为汝姊夫言之，可充陪宾 - "今我肆中客多" means "now there are many guests in my shop," "我为汝姊夫言之" means "I will tell my brother-in-law," and "可充陪宾" means "you can serve as a companion to guests."
  • 不过饮食谈笑，无他难也 - "不过" means "just," "饮食谈笑" means "eating and chatting," and "无他难也" means "nothing else is difficult."
  • 且可得数两劳金 - "且" means "and," "可得" means "can get," "数两" means "a few taels," and "劳金" means "labor money."
  • 三官善谈论，意甚惬 - "善谈论" means "good at talking," and "意甚惬" means "he was very pleased."
  • 其姊夫试使见客，訚訚如也 - "试使见客" means "tried to make him meet guests," "訚訚如也" means "he was polite and composed."
  • 词多诌媚，人皆悦之 - "词多诌媚" means "his words were flattering," and "人皆悦之" means "everyone was pleased with him."
  • 游山观剧，罔不与共者 - "游山观剧" means "visiting mountains and watching plays," and "罔不与共者" means "he always went along with them."

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 壬戌 - A specific year in the Chinese calendar, corresponding to 1902 AD.
  • 武林 - An ancient name for Hangzhou, a city known for its cultural and economic significance.
  • 染坊 - A dyeing workshop, a common feature in traditional Chinese towns.
  • 练色 - The process of dyeing silk, a significant industry in ancient China.
  • 杵声与俚歌相答 - Folk songs and the sounds of pounding, reflecting the daily life and culture of the time.
  • 落寞小肆 - A small and unremarkable shop, indicating a modest social status.
  • 忠厚 - A virtue emphasizing honesty and kindness, highly valued in traditional Chinese society.
  • 完姻 - Getting married, a significant life event in traditional Chinese culture.
  • 食指日繁 - Increasing number of dependents, reflecting the growth of a family.
  • 郁卒 - Dying of depression, a common cause of death in traditional Chinese literature.
  • 游手好闲 - Loafing around, a negative trait often criticized in traditional Chinese society.
  • 陪宾 - Serving as a companion to guests, a social role in traditional Chinese settings.
  • 劳金 - Labor money, a form of payment for services rendered.
  • 訚訚如也 - Polite and composed, a desirable quality in social interactions.
  • 诌媚 - Flattering, a behavior often criticized in traditional Chinese literature.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 有高客非三官不欢 - Colloquial expression meaning 'there is a high-ranking guest who will not be happy without the three officials.'
  • 竟成莫逆交 - Means 'they eventually became close friends.'
  • 无事时，客聚博 - Means 'when there was nothing to do, the guests gathered to gamble.'
  • 以无钱辞 - Means 'refused due to lack of money.'
  • 为收藏 - Means 'to keep it for safekeeping.'
  • 闻汝博有起色 - Means 'I hear that your gambling has been going well.'
  • 但无本之事，依人为活 - Means 'but gambling without capital, relying on others for a living.'
  • 一朝失手，何以对人 - Means 'if you lose one day, how will you face others?'
  • 藉此经营本业 - Means 'use this to manage your business.'
  • 或有起家之日 - Means 'there might be a day when you can build a family.'
  • 即为恒产，岂不妙哉 - Means 'if it becomes a permanent property, wouldn't that be wonderful?'
  • 唯唯 - A polite response indicating agreement.
  • 与高客言，欣然与银如数 - Means 'he agreed with the high-ranking guest and happily gave the silver as requested.'
  • 赴泖河，投行家置靛 - Means 'he went to Maohu to deal with a merchant and buy indigo.'
  • 贬价甚廉 - Means 'the price was very low.'
  • 尚无人过而问也 - Means 'no one had come to ask about it.'
  • 自叹无成 - Means 'he sighed at his failure.'
  • 以再商含糊复之 - Means 'he responded ambiguously after further negotiation.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 高客 - Refers to a high-ranking guest, possibly a noble or official.
  • 三官 - Refers to three officials or people of some importance.
  • 博 - Refers to gambling, a common pastime in ancient China.
  • 泖河 - A river in China, possibly a specific location mentioned in the text.
  • 靛 - A dye made from the indigo plant, commonly used in ancient China for coloring fabrics.

  • 三官以实告 - San Guan (a person or group) tells the truth.
  • 客无奈 - The guest is helpless or has no choice.
  • 愿先得二百金作归计 - The guest wishes to receive two hundred gold coins first for his return.
  • 余银立券事后取偿 - The remaining silver will be settled with a contract after the matter is completed.
  • 主人作中 - The host acts as a mediator or intermediary.
  • 互相交易 - They engage in mutual transactions.
  • 各执契券 - Each holds a contract.
  • 客先行 - The guest leaves first.

  • 安得如许银 - How can such a large amount of silver be obtained?
  • 不必存此妄想 - There is no need to harbor such unrealistic hopes.
  • 姑舍是仍旧贯可也 - Perhaps it is best to set aside this matter and continue as before.

  • 怀抱不开 - He is not in a good mood or is depressed.
  • 静坐极闷郁 - He sits quietly, extremely bored and depressed.
  • 六百金小事 - Six hundred gold coins is a small matter.
  • 何必为难 - There is no need to make it difficult.
  • 归即贷汝 - Upon returning, I will lend you the money.
  • 鼓兴畅谈 - They talk enthusiastically and freely.

  • 其姊夫阻之 - His sister-in-law objects.
  • 与不与在我 - Whether to give or not is up to me.
  • 还不还听渠 - Whether to repay or not is up to him.
  • 与尔无干 - It has nothing to do with you.
  • 毋预他人事也 - Do not interfere in other people's affairs.
  • 愧谢而退 - She apologizes and withdraws.
